Warish Hussain

Environmental Analyst

Turning climate & spatial data into insights | GIS | Remote Sensing | ESG | Sustainability | Climate Risk

docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
I am an environmental professional with a background in environmental science, sustainability, and geospatial analysis. I work on understanding climate and environmental challenges through GIS, remote sensing, and climate data analysis, with interests spanning climate risk assessment, urban ecology, heat stress, and nature-based solutions. I use tools and methods including Python, GIS, remote sensing, climate datasets such as ERA5 and CMIP, and sustainability frameworks to support data-driven environmental decision-making. I am currently seeking opportunities in Climate Risk Analysis, GIS and Geospatial Analytics, Sustainability Consulting, and Environmental Data Analysis in Delhi-NCR or remote roles.

View My Projects Download CV

docs.title: Spatial Monitoring and Analysis of Haloacetic Acids in Drinking Water Using GIS: A Case Study of Jamia Nagar, New Delhi
docs.text:
Ahmed, S., Mazhar, M.A. & Hussain, W.

Environmental Monitoring and Assessment, 198, 46 (2026)

Read Paper

Abstract

This study focuses on the spatial monitoring and analysis of haloacetic acids (HAAs) in drinking water using GIS techniques, with Jamia Nagar, New Delhi as the study area. The research investigates the spatial variation of HAA concentrations and their implications for drinking water quality and public health.

An interactive web map visualizing industrial plant locations across Jammu & Kashmir, built using Python, Leafmap, and Folium with OpenStreetMap base tiles and clickable popups.

Python Leafmap Folium OpenStreetMap

Carbon Stock Mapping — JMI Campus

Machine learning-based carbon stock estimation for Jamia Millia Islamia campus using Sentinel-1 SAR, Sentinel-2 optical imagery, GEDI LiDAR, and Random Forest regression in Google Earth Engine.

Google Earth Engine Sentinel-1 Sentinel-2 Random Forest GEDI

View Project →

docs.title: Carbon Stock Mapping of JMI Campus Using Google Earth Engine
Overview
docs.text: A machine learning-based carbon stock estimation study for Jamia Millia Islamia (JMI) campus, New Delhi, using multi-source satellite data and Google Earth Engine. The study integrates SAR, optical, and LiDAR-derived canopy height data to estimate above-ground biomass and carbon stocks across the urban campus.

docs.title: Tools & Technologies
docs.text:
• Google Earth Engine (GEE) — cloud-based geospatial processing
  • Sentinel-1 SAR — VV/VH polarization for vegetation structure
  • Sentinel-2 Optical — spectral bands, NDVI, EVI
  • GEDI LiDAR — canopy height reference data (rh98)
  • SRTM DEM — elevation and slope terrain features
  • Dynamic World — land cover classification and vegetation masking
  • Random Forest Regression — machine learning model (500 trees)

docs.title: Phase 1 — Data Collection & Preprocessing
docs.text:
• Loaded post-monsoon Sentinel-1 SAR data (Oct–Dec 2023) to avoid cloud contamination
  • Computed percentile composites (25th, 50th, 75th) for robust SAR statistics
  • Applied cloud masking on Sentinel-2 SR imagery
  • Derived terrain variables (elevation, slope) from SRTM DEM
  • Used Dynamic World to mask out buildings and roads from analysis

docs.title: Phase 2 — Feature Engineering
docs.text:
Combined 18 predictor variables:

Category Features SAR (Sentinel-1) VH, VV median + IQR texture Optical (Sentinel-2) B2–B12 spectral bands Vegetation Indices NDVI, EVI Terrain Elevation, Slope

docs.title: Phase 3 — Machine Learning Model
docs.text:
• Trained Random Forest Regression (500 trees) using GEDI canopy height as reference
  • Split data: 70% training / 30% testing
  • Compared against Gradient Tree Boosting and CART models

docs.title: Phase 4 — Carbon Stock Estimation
docs.text:
• Converted predicted canopy height → Above-Ground Biomass (AGB) using allometric equations
  • Applied IPCC default carbon fraction (0.47) to estimate carbon stocks
  • Calculated total carbon storage in tonnes across the campus

docs.title: Key Results
docs.text:
• Generated wall-to-wall canopy height map at 10m resolution
  • Estimated Above-Ground Biomass (Mg/ha) across vegetated areas
  • Calculated total carbon stock (tonnes) for JMI campus
  • Produced exportable GeoTIFF outputs for canopy height, AGB, and carbon maps

docs.title: GEE Code
docs.text:
// Sample: Carbon Stock Estimation from Canopy Height
// Using IPCC default carbon fraction (0.47)

var agb = canopyHeightMasked.expression(
  '10.0 * pow(height, 1.3)', {
    'height': canopyHeightMasked.select('canopy_height')
  }).rename('AGB_Mg_ha');

var carbonStock = agb.multiply(0.47).rename('Carbon_Mg_ha');

docs.title: Industrial Plant Location Mapping — Jammu & Kashmir
Overview
docs.text: An interactive web map visualizing the locations of industrial plants across Jammu & Kashmir, built using Python, Leafmap, and Folium. The map includes clickable markers with plant names and addresses sourced from OSM and government datasets.

docs.title: Tools & Technologies
docs.text:
• Python — data processing and map generation
  • Leafmap / Folium — interactive map creation
  • OpenStreetMap — base map tiles
  • Leaflet.js — web mapping library

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Interactive markers with popup information for each plant
  • Plant name and address displayed on click
  • Responsive web map embeddable in any browser

docs.title: Skills Demonstrated
docs.text:
• Geospatial data processing with Python
  • Interactive web map development
  • OSM data integration
  • Spatial data visualization
